## 11. Risks and Mitigations

### 11.1 Risk Matrix

| ID | Risk | Prob. | Impact | Exposure | Mitigation |
|----|------|-------|--------|----------|------------|
| R1 | Seem generic | High | Fatal | **Critical** | Terminology + realistic seed + consultant |
| R2 | Slow solver | Medium | High | **High** | Timeout + cache + limit data |
| R3 | Unanswered questions | Medium | High | **High** | FAQ + 2h consultant |
| R4 | Bug during demo | Medium | High | **High** | Fallback + exhaustive rehearsal |
| R5 | Scope creep | High | Medium | **High** | PO as guardian, closed scope |
| R6 | Team without domain knowledge | High | Medium | **High** | Mandatory immersion week 1 |
| R7 | API/Solver integration fails | Low | High | **Medium** | Mock solver as fallback |
| R8 | Designer doesn't understand domain | Medium | Medium | **Medium** | Designer participates in immersion |

### 11.2 Contingency Plans

#### R1: Seem Generic

**Triggers:** Negative validation feedback, incorrect terms in UI.

**Actions:**
1. Pause development
2. Hire railway consultant (4h)
3. Review all terminology
4. Redo seed with consultant data

#### R4: Bug During Demo

**Triggers:** Crash in rehearsal.

**Actions:**
1. Identify failure scenarios
2. Create fallback screens (PNG/PDF)
3. Alternative script avoiding unstable areas
4. Presenter trained to work around

#### R5: Scope Creep

**Triggers:** Stakeholder asks "just one more feature".

**Actions:**
1. PO documents request
2. Assesses timeline impact
3. Trade-off: "To include X, we remove Y"
4. Documented formal decision

---

## 12. Presentation Script

### 12.1 Structure (15 minutes)

| Min | Section | Action | Objective |
|-----|---------|--------|-----------|
| 0-1 | Opening | Title slide | Professionalism |
| 1-3 | Context | "We understand your problem" | Credibility |
| 3-5 | Dashboard | KPIs, alerts, overview | First impression |
| 5-7 | Map | Trains, depots, clustering | Visual wow |
| 7-9 | Balancing | Chart, outliers | Solve main pain point |
| 9-11 | Allocation | Recommendation + justification | Engine working |
| 11-13 | Simulation | Interactive what-if | Client interacts |
| 13-14 | Transparency | "Not a black box" | Trust |
| 14-15 | Closing | Next steps | Call to action |

### 12.2 Script by Section

#### Opening (0-1 min)

> "Good morning. Today we'll show how MMS solves the challenge of balancing fleet utilization while maintaining operational availability."

#### Context (1-3 min)

> "We spoke with sector operators and identified three main pain points:
> 1. Over-running trains degrade faster, increasing maintenance cost
> 2. Under-running trains represent idle capital
> 3. Manual planning in Excel doesn't scale
>
> MMS addresses all three points."

#### Dashboard (3-5 min)

**Action:** Show dashboard screen.

> "This is the executive view. In seconds you see:
> - 98 of 112 trains available
> - Km variance at 18% — above the ideal 10%
> - 2 depots near capacity
>
> Red here indicates action needed."

#### Map (5-7 min)

**Action:** Navigate to map, zoom in/out.

> "Here we see each train's current position. Zoom out groups automatically. Clicking on a depot, we see stationed trains and occupancy."

#### Balancing (7-9 min)

**Action:** Show distribution chart.

> "This chart shows km distribution. See these 5 trains well above average — they're priority candidates for stopping. And these 4 below — we should allocate more."

#### Allocation (9-11 min)

**Action:** Execute live allocation.

> "Let's allocate a train for tomorrow's IC 501 service."
> [Selects service, clicks Recommend]
> "The system suggests train UTE-3400-012. Why? See the justification:
> - 12% below average — needs to run more
> - Ends in Porto, which has maintenance slot in 3 days
> - Aptitude confirmed for the line"

#### Simulation (11-13 min)

**Action:** Create scenario, adjust, compare.

> "What if we want to test another strategy? We create a parallel scenario..."
> [Shows side-by-side]
> "We see the alternative reduces variance by 3% but increases dead running by 8%. The decision is yours, with data."

#### Transparency (13-14 min)

> "Important: MMS is not a black box. Every recommendation shows the why. You maintain control, the system helps you decide."

#### Closing (14-15 min)

> "In summary: visible balancing, intelligent allocation with justification, simulation for informed decision. Questions?"

### 12.3 Prepared FAQ

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| "Integrates with SAP?" | "The architecture supports it. For production, we connect via standard API." |
| "What if solver is slow?" | "30s timeout, fallback to last valid solution." |
| "Works offline?" | "Web-based, needs connection. Offline mode on roadmap." |
| "Considers crew?" | "Current model treats as simplified constraint. Full integration in phase 2." |
| "How many trains supported?" | "Demo validated for 100. Architecture scales to 500+." |
| "How is km updated?" | "Telemetry integration or batch import from EAM." |

### 12.4 Plan B (If Something Fails)

| Failure | Action |
|---------|--------|
| Screen doesn't load | "While it loads, let me explain..." (fallback slide) |
| Solver timeout | "The system is calculating complex scenarios. Here's a pre-computed result." |
| Total crash | PDF slides with screenshots of each screen |

---

## 13. Appendices

### Appendix A: Complete Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| Allocation | Assignment of train to service |
| Aptitude | Train qualification for route/service type |
| Bogie | Structure with wheels under the car |
| Consist | Composition of units forming train |
| Dead running | Empty train movement (no passengers/cargo) |
| Depot | Parking and/or maintenance location |
| ECM | Entity in Charge of Maintenance — maintenance responsible |
| EAM | Enterprise Asset Management — asset management system |
| HM | Heavy Maintenance |
| HMD | Heavy Maintenance Depot |
| LMD | Light Maintenance Depot |
| Married pair | Mechanically inseparable train units |
| MTTR | Mean Time To Repair |
| Overhaul | General revision (most complete) |
| PM | Preventive Maintenance |
| Rolling stock | Trains, locomotives, wagons |
| Service | A specific route in the timetable |
| Shunting | Train maneuvering in yard |
| Timetable | Service schedule |
| Wheelset | Wheel + axle assembly |

### Appendix B: Domain FAQ (For Team)

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| Why not call it "car" or "vehicle"? | Technical term is "rolling stock". |
| What's the difference between LMD and HMD? | LMD does light maintenance (PM1, PM2). HMD does heavy (overhaul). |
| What is "dead running"? | Train running empty. Has fuel/energy cost and wear without revenue. |
| Why is km balancing important? | Uniform wear = equal lifecycle = synchronized new fleet purchase = savings. |
| What is "consist"? | The composition of units forming a train. Can change. |
| What are "married pairs"? | Units that cannot be separated (permanent mechanical connection). |

### Appendix C: Visual References

**Systems to study:**
1. Railnova Railfleet — https://www.railnova.eu/
2. DELMIA Quintiq — https://www.3ds.com/products-services/delmia/products/quintiq/
